mission.title: argo: Add option to create one-off job from cron job
mission.description: Add option to create one-off job from cron job. Requested by 163+ users.

steps.title: Check current argo deployment
steps.description:
Verify your argo version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
helm list -n argo 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working argo installation.

steps.title: Review argo configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant argo configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get configmap -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argo
```
Add a button to create a one-off job from a cron job.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Add option to create one-off job from cron job
steps.description:
Basically, the result of performing a "kubectl create job --from=cronjob/foo foo" is : k8s creating a job resource immediately.
The job manifest is yet another k8s resource.

My suggestion: handling the imperative "kubectl create" result as a declarative representation of this one-time job resource
```yaml
apiVersion: batch/v1
kind: CronJob
metadata:
  ...
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n argo -l app.kubernetes.io/name=argo
kubectl get events -n argo --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Add option to create one-off job from cron job" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Basically, the result of performing a "kubectl create job --from=cronjob/foo foo" is : k8s creating a job resource immediately.
The job manifest is yet another k8s resource.

My suggestion: handling the imperative "kubectl create" result as a declarative representation of this one-time job resource and dealing with it in a regular GitOps way.
